codeReview.summary: Implementation is solid and covers all required functionality. Admin login page at app/admin/login/page.tsx with email+password form and SAE-A Trading branding. Admin dashboard at app/admin/dashboard/page.tsx with 4 stat cards (users, products, events, orders) fetched from GET /api/admin/stats. Admin layout with dark sidebar navigation linking to Dashboard, Products, Events, Users, Orders sections plus logout. Middleware properly protects /admin/* routes: unauthenticated users redirect to /admin/login, non-admin users redirect to /. Admin logout via signOut() clears session. Tests are comprehensive (19 tests) covering API auth/authorization, stats endpoint, login UI, and dashboard rendering. Two non-blocking issues identified.

issues.description: After successful signIn(), the page does router.push('/admin/dashboard') without checking the user's role. If a regular (non-admin) user logs in via /admin/login, they get a successful signIn response, then the client navigates to /admin/dashboard, only to be redirected to / by middleware. The user sees no error message explaining they lack admin access. Security is enforced by middleware, but UX is poor — the user gets silently redirected with no feedback. A post-login role check (e.g., fetch session and verify role=admin, or check via /api/admin/stats 403) would improve the experience.

The authorized callback returns true for all /admin routes (line 55-58), intentionally bypassing withAuth's built-in redirect to delegate to the middleware function. This works but is a fragile pattern — it means the middleware function must handle all admin auth logic including the unauthenticated case (token=null). If a future developer modifies the middleware function without understanding this delegation, admin routes could become unprotected. A comment explaining this design choice would help maintainability.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Admin pages use <a> tags for navigation links (e.g., in sidebar and dashboard stat cards/quick actions) instead of Next.js <Link> components. This causes full page reloads on every navigation within the admin panel. User-facing pages likely use <Link> for client-side navigation. AGENTS.md doesn't specify whether admin pages should use <Link>, but this is a Next.js best practice that affects performance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/admin/layout.tsx:68 uses <a key={item.name} href={item.href}> for sidebar nav. app/admin/dashboard/page.tsx:105 uses <a key={card.title} href={card.link}> for stat cards and <a href='/admin/products'> for quick actions. Compare to typical Next.js patterns which use <Link> from 'next/link'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The admin login page uses the shared NextAuth credentials provider without any admin-specific role verification after login. The feature description mentioned 'use NextAuth but check role=admin, or separate admin session logic' — the implementation chose the shared NextAuth path but defers role checking entirely to middleware, meaning the login page itself doesn't differentiate between admin and regular users. This is a design decision worth documenting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/admin/login/page.tsx:51 calls signIn('credentials', {emailOrPhone, password, redirect: false}) — same as regular user login. Role check happens only in middleware.ts:30-36.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The admin-worker skill specifies TDD (step 3: 'Write tests first, run pnpm test to confirm tests fail'). The worker transcript and handoff show tests were written and all pass, but from the transcript skeleton it's unclear whether strict red-green-refactor was followed. The handoff's skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true with no deviations, so the worker claims compliance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill SKILL.md step 3: 'Run pnpm test to confirm tests fail (red phase)'. Handoff skillFeedback: {followedProcedure: true, deviations: []}.

summary: The admin-auth-and-dashboard feature passes review. All required functionality is implemented: admin login page with branding, dashboard with 4 stat cards, sidebar navigation, GET /api/admin/stats endpoint with auth/role checks, middleware protection of /admin/* routes with proper redirects, and admin logout. 19 tests cover auth, stats API (including 401/403/500 cases), login UI, and dashboard rendering. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) the login page doesn't verify the user's role after signIn, causing a silent redirect for non-admin users, and (2) the middleware delegation pattern for admin routes could benefit from documentation. Additionally, admin navigation uses <a> tags instead of Next.js <Link> components, causing unnecessary full-page reloads.
